_meta.data: global_alpha_dataset_1h_2pct.csv (rebuilt after Tier 2 features confirmed harmful)
_meta.rationale_overview: OOF analysis showed per-symbol heterogeneity: 2 symbols with strong edge (SOL, DOGE), 1 with weak large-sample edge (XRP), all others at random or negative. Skipping zero-edge symbols outperforms a single global threshold, since the aggregate 52.6% WR @ thr=0.50+funding-filter is diluted by negative-edge symbols (BTC, ETH, BNB, LINK, ADA) and propped up by SOL/DOGE.
_meta.stability_caveat: P3 (most recent ~year) showed only 38.8% WR @ thr=0.50 + funding filter (n=67), vs P2's 60.6%. Bulk of edge sits in middle period. Size positions small; revisit if live edge drifts below 50% over a meaningful sample.
_meta.tier2_status: session and cross-asset features tried and reverted: D-only WR=35.7%, E-only WR=47.3% on 3y OOF -> both negative additions

_default.rationale: No validated per-symbol edge; threshold>1 means never enters. Must be explicitly listed below to trade.

SOLUSDT.rationale: Strongest per-symbol edge in 3y OOF: 60.3% WR over 141 trades. Funding filter not needed (in fact weakens trade count). Highest priority symbol.

DOGEUSDT.rationale: Second strongest edge: 55.6% WR over 126 trades. No funding filter.

XRPUSDT.rationale: Weak but high-volume edge: 52.3% WR over 1315 trades. Largest sample of any symbol; the most statistically reliable edge even though per-trade EPnL is small.

AVAXUSDT.rationale: Marginal: 51.8% WR over 137 trades. Borderline-significant; included with conservative threshold but expect lower SR than SOL/DOGE.
